Detailed Description

Uniquable metadata node.

A uniquable metadata node. This contains the basic functionality for implementing sub-types of MDNode that can be uniqued like constants.

There is limited support for RAUW at construction time. At construction time, if any operands are an instance of MDNodeFwdDecl (or another unresolved UniquableMDNode, which indicates an MDNodeFwdDecl in its path), the node itself will be unresolved. As soon as all operands become resolved, it will drop RAUW support permanently.

If an unresolved node is part of a cycle, resolveCycles() needs to be called on some member of the cycle when each MDNodeFwdDecl has been removed.

Definition at line 715 of file Metadata.h.

Constructor & Destructor Documentation

◆ UniquableMDNode()

UniquableMDNode::UniquableMDNode ( LLVMContext & C,
unsigned ID,
ArrayRef< Metadata * > Vals,
bool AllowRAUW )
protected

Create a new node.

If AllowRAUW, then if any operands are unresolved support RAUW. RAUW will be dropped once all operands have been resolved (or if resolveCycles() is called).

◆ isResolved()

bool llvm::UniquableMDNode::isResolved ( ) const
inline

Check whether any operands are forward declarations.

Returns true as long as any operands (or their operands, etc.) are MDNodeFwdDecl.

As forward declarations are resolved, their containers should get resolved automatically. However, if this (or one of its operands) is involved in a cycle, resolveCycles() needs to be called explicitly.

Definition at line 752 of file Metadata.h.

Referenced by MapMetadataImpl(), and resolveCycles().

◆ resolveCycles()

void UniquableMDNode::resolveCycles ( )

Resolve cycles.

Once all forward declarations have been resolved, force cycles to be resolved.

Precondition
No operands (or operands' operands, etc.) are MDNodeFwdDecl.
